name: Louis, E. Winfrey
clubs: University editor of The University Weekly, '08
description: "Prof." was the man that made the German language. He is not an entire success as a ladies' man, and does not care anyway. He is going to be a language Prof. and knows how to "flunk" one gracefully already.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_01.png

name: M., E. Tucker
description: He is big and robust, "Hale fellow well met." Quite a musician and doesn't run around much; still he has a pretty good time.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_01.png

name: W., C. Murphy
description: Murphy is an Irishman, in name only, for no Irishman ever combed his hair as does this man. He doesn't use a rat, either. Mr. Wallace Murphy is a good boy, if he is from Ft. Smith. He intends to be a preacher, or a lawyer, or something of the sort.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_01.png

name: Hannan, H. Holtzclaw
clubs: Works in Commandant's office to escape drill
Assistant engineer of the Military department
description: Long and lean. He is an agricultural fiend, can tell a corn stalk from a cotton blossom from afar. P. S. - "Coldslaw" does not use curling irons.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_01.png

name: Arthur, C. Davis
description: Arthur is a good student; in fact, such a good student that he hasn't taken time to have his hair cut in the last ten years.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_01.png

name: Russel, E. Lambert
clubs: Leader of the German band and first aid to falling stars
description: He is the tallest Senior in captivity. He is fond of Dr. Brough, because of the latter's frequent reference to "Suncrowned men," carrier of the big stick, sometimes called a baton. He always endeavors to "beat the band."
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_01.png

name: Phil, McNemer B. A.
description: "Mack" is a great big fellow who does our kicking when we play football. He also knows how to play baseball, so the Seniors say.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_02.png

name: Myrtie, Farish L. I.
motto: "To learn when I was a Freshman, I would not, and then when I got old I could not."
clubs: Treasurer of Executive Board of Carnall Hall, '07
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_02.png

name: Guy A., Watkins
description: Guy is a civil engineer. He has lately been studying on a plan by which he can make a submarine airship. It is useless to say that if he succeeds in this that his reputation as an inventor will be established.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_02.png

name: Frances, Douglas
clubs: Vice President Y. W. C. A. '08-'09
description: She is very fond of books, although she dislikes to be called a "college grind," so much so I have been told that she has assumed a love affair. At least she has taken up the study of Bertha M. Clay. She goes about her work with a suspicious smile and is frequently heard singing, "Just a little rocking chair and you."
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_02.png

name: Truman D., Williamson
motto: "It is," he says, "self-constructing, self-repairing, self-oiling and a self-operating machine."
description: T. D. is a fiend after electrical apparatus. He has invented an electrical machine that will doubtless revolutionize the industrial world.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_02.png

name: Jewell, Machen
description: For some unaccountable reason no remarks were written about this noble Senior. If you want to know the reason ask her.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_02.png

name: Woods, John P.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_03.png

name: Ara, Mitchell B. A.
motto: "You can never hitch your wagon to a star if you keep your eyes lowered on your pony."
description: A wee bit lassie, very "cute," though often accused of the sin of deception. She goes about with her coy smile and neat little curls trying to play "sweet sixteen," but you can't fool us. She also has the reputation of being a graceful rider; however, there is one peculiar thing about her—she always carries her pony in her pocket.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_04.png

name: C. R., Rhodes
description: Quarterback "Dusty" has followed the '09 team to victory three times. He is little but loud.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_04.png

name: Nell, Coleman
description: You can never fail to recognize this dignified little Senior, known as the "Hermit" of the Dorm. It has long been a perplexing question to the matron as to how she will induce her to take enough exercise. She seems to take no notice of things about her. The matron has finally decided to place her under a very strong woman who will teach her how to play jokes, especially on the Juniors.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_04.png

name: Garland, Hurt
description: "Miss Hurt" is an autocratic gentleman who presides over the University Weekly board. He is a leader in society and likes to be among beautiful women, but doesn't bring his heart when he comes to school.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_04.png

name: Bessie, Carnall
description: Of the many promising characters at the 'Varsity this year, Miss Carnall is the most talented. Her voice is something to be wondered at. She usually chooses the midnight hour in which to practice her songs, in order that at that quiet time she may be able to distinguish the "mellow tones." In her zeal she forgot the comfort of her neighbors, and it became necessary for her to move to the first floor. She is now dying her hair in preparation for a trip to the Cincinnati Conservatory.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_04.png

name: Reed, Alice
motto: "But there is more in me than thou understandest."
description: Alice's good humor has won her great love. She smiles so much that she has often been accused of flirting. She is very modest in her aspirations, not caring for woman's rights. She is also very domestic in her tastes, and is a firm believer in the old saying, "The shortest way to a man's heart is through his stomach."
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_05.png

name: Blair, Dan
description: Short, sour and sassy, is an ardent lover of "Preps." Can be seen fondling them at all times.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_05.png

name: Trent, Bess
description: Bess is Myrtle Miller's chum; they stick to each other like a sick kitten to a hot brick. You never see one without the other. They are very much alike.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_05.png

name: Gibson, R. C.
description: Gibson is a human question box. He can discuss any question relating to English, Economics, History, Latin or most anything else. His ideas are always guaranteed to be original. He is short and square, and wears a smile which is adjustable for the use of either old or young ladies. He doesn't mind talking to strangers.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_05.png

name: Miller, Myrtle
motto: "I care for nobody, no not I, if nobody cares for me."
description: Myrtle is the other part of the Miller-Trent chumship. They form a dead "crush"—a crush is a conjunction of two persons having a strong affinity for each other, and when they get tangled up in each other's arms, it is hard to tell which is the mushier, more impressionable substance.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_05.png

name: Cash, C. C.
description: The lion of the Weekly, his teachers see in him the elements of a Mark Twain. He has a very strong affinity for Freshman girls and so frequent are his visits to Carnall Hall that Mrs. Park has consulted the Dean about charging him rent for parlor space, and the wear and tear of the furniture. Under the magnanimous name of man-killer he even tried to get a room at Carnall Hall.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_06.png

name: Lester, Roy B.
clubs: Member of Y. M. C. A. Quartette
description: He is a vocalist, organist, pianist, and a humorist, but not a socialist. Now I guess you know who LESTER is.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_06.png

name: Snell, Merrill
description: An engineer who has finished his course in three years. This shows that he is one of the brightest members of his class. The latest problem he has been working on is that it is possible to construct an elevated railroad under the Mississippi River.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_06.png

name: Pulley, Cameron H.
description: A nice, quiet fellow who always wears Sunday-go-to-meetin' clothes. Jim is a real, true ladies' man sometimes, but he will go down in history as a man "too great to be president."
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_06.png

name: Tillman, Fred A.
description: Fred is the last man in the University that we would have thought would have fallen in love, but Cupid's fiery darts have even reached his hard, hard heart. He is now a slave to a habit, love.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_06.png

name: Rhodes, Joe W.
description: Joe is built square, all dimensions are rather short. He thinks he knows how to play football, but he gets the game confused with prize fighting. He has a patented walk and is day keeper of the Commandant.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_07.png

name: Grubbs, William W.
description: Grubbs is not old, he just appears to be. It is whispered that "Daddy" has been clearing a convenient place on top of his head preparatory to moving up his face. What the cause of his dissatisfaction is, is not known, for you can see that he is not bad looking.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_07.png

name: Wilson, C. N.
description: C. N. is another engineer who is supposed to graduate in three years, but it is doubtful whether he will make it or not. He is a little gaseous, in fact we are afraid to strike a match within his vicinity. Recently he has patented a new hot air furnace.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_07.png

name: Shipley, John L.
description: John L. is not as fisty as the other John L. S. was. He is a quiet, good-natured and unassuming "cuss," who never says a bad word and very seldom any other kind. He is always springing surprises when one least expects them.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_07.png

name: Allen, Estes
description: Some malicious Junior once hinted that he was not a Senior. He wishes us to make mention of the fact that he is a Senior.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_07.png

name: Tucker, Justin R.
description: He is a little man with keen, black eyes; loves to hunt quail on posted land. He toots a horn and can always be found on the band wagon. He is seldom seen after nightfall.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_08.png

name: Ketchem, Annie
motto: "If you do not see her you will hear her."
description: It is a serious thing for a girl to get afflicted with the "smiles." Annie has a bad case of them. As she grows older, she seems to realize her condition, and has been seen several times trying to close her mouth. It seems to be the reaction of a serious love affair, as she is often heard saying to herself, "Men are not always what they seem."
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_08.png

name: Blair, Therom C.
description: T. C. is a blushing youth of sixteen years, or more. Blair is noted for his dainty mouth and his company (female?). His long suit is not going with the fair sex. He spurns them. In other words he is not for them. Blair is a big Y. M. C. A. man; perhaps this accounts for his disposition.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_08.png

name: Nunn, Lucie
description: You all know Lucy, at least, you have heard of her. She talks in her sleep and often disturbs her roommate with indistinct murmurs about "preachers." Her most confidential friends say she is thinking seriously of changing her religion. She, of course, denies this in her waking hours, but prevarication covers a multitude of sins.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_08.png

name: Chamberlain, Roy
description: Bashful, modest, very coy. Roy can't stay away from Mount Nord. He has an artistic temperament, reveling in Maupassant and "The Billboard." He has never loved—in vain.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_08.png

name: Norton, Delbert A.
description: Senator wears a cap on his head and a twenty-five dollar meerschaum in his mouth. He is a musician and when selling CARDINALS an orator. The Senator always sheds his top hair in the spring—watch for the sign.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_09.png

name: Hixson, Guy E.
description: Guy is a football enthusiast and a good player. He is one of these athletic fellows who likes to be idolized. But "Guy" will not let but one girl at a time worship at his shrine.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_09.png

name: Warterfield, Elgin A.
description: "Waterbury" wears a pompadour. He is from Oklahoma and 'tis whispered that he is 4-17th Creek Indian. He debated his home university this year. Elgin loves the fruit (not the after effects) of the chafing dish. He knows where to go, too.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_09.png

name: Dulaney, John J.
clubs: Joint winner in the Drury Debate, '08
description: John is a simple man. He is aspiring, studious and subtle. He is the incumbent of the President's chair of the Senior class. He knows how to make a slate. He was not in the Marathon race, but should have been. He believes in the divine right of kings and that "The state can do no wrong."
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_09.png

name: Davis, O. L.
description: He gets out of drill.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_09.png

name: Nesbit, William E.
description: If you see a nice looking boy, rather slender, walking around with his head in the air, observing the rabble through well cleaned glasses, that's "Bill" Nesbit.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_10.png

name: Gibson, Ruby
motto: "Love makes fools of us all, big and little."
description: Her wit might be likened to that of Solomon. She is a great lover of nature and spends much of her time strolling about, observing the beauty and grandeur of the "Woods." Her musical talent is as great as that of Orpheus, for although she has not yet induced the mice and children to follow her, the forest seems to be walking in her footsteps.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_10.png

name: Womack, Richard E.
description: He is a worthy man if he is a major. He is the only officer of the day who has successfully worked a bluff. WOMACK is a connoisseur in good bed-time stories and lullaby songs. He is a grammarian of "purest ray serene."
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_10.png

name: Ellis, Forrest
description: It is a puzzle to many of her classmates why she looks so sad. Some say she is growing a mouth and chin, or is becoming to be a lawyer, as she aspires to that profession. None of us doubt it, considering her love for Economics. Dewey is her favorite author, and she also expresses great admiration for Seligman.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_10.png

name: Oates, Max Bruce
description: Bruce is a football enthusiast and an all around athlete. He was the Senior left half back. OATES is the southern salesman of the Century Books of Facts, and can sell to every unfortunate being on whom he sees fit to visit his wrath.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_10.png

name: Sherrill, John A.
description: "Johnnie" is the business end of the Weekly. He says that the only thing that the Weekly needs to make it the best college paper in the South is an extra couple of sheets of advertising.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_11.png

name: Moore, George J.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_11.png

name: Thetford, A.
description: Although he is a Senior, it took many until after mid-term exams to find out that he was not a "Prep."
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_11.png

name: Ford, David L.
description: "Dave" has been an orator, a footballist (indirectly), and was at one time an editor. He is now living down a past reputation. Remember him in your prayers. Special to the Publishers: Through oversight there was no mention of the fact that he was Matron of the Dormitories for three months.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_11.png

name: Mehaffy, James
description: It is easy to see that he is from Henderson. "He is President."
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_11.png

name: Bunn, James B.
description: Bunn is something of a sport. He says he is cut out for a lawyer. Uses Foley's Face Food. When interviewed, Mr. Bunn said: "For twelve years my complexion was dark and muddy, but after using Foley's Face Cream for two days I could see a change and two boxes affected a complete cure. I always keep it on my dresser." For sale at all druggists; two sizes, 25 and 50 cents.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_12.png

name: Bailey, Wilbur S.
description: Bailey is a good captain, even though he didn't get to be major. He is a quiet, unobtrusive fellow and was never known to sport.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_12.png

name: Wootton, Leonard L.
description: Leonard has a thirty-second degree in society. He is professor of "Carpentry," and official distributor of campus tickets. "Sunshine" Wootton, as he is known by some, is the man who invented athletic gravy. He is the man behind the typewriter, the living, pulsating heart of the institution and the "Prep" Bureau of Information.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_12.png

name: Allen, Joe C.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of the Dormitory, '08-'09
description: "Joe" represents the effects of association. He is naturally a very religious sort of fellow and is president of the Y. M. C. A., but has never broken himself of the room stacking habit. He likes all the girls, but loves Nunn.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_12.png

name: Hawkins, Frank C.
description: Frank says that football is too strenuous. However, he believes in Roosevelt's other theories. He loves Latin, Greek, Economics, Athletic Gravy and Miss —. He is the author of "How to Live Easy."
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_12.png

name: Nichols, Elizabeth
description: A demure little maid, whose egotism and self-righteousness has deceived many. A friend becoming curious about her many prayers, found her one evening kneeling before her mirror admiring her likeness through a pair of opera glasses, earnestly repeating the prayer, "I thank thee that I am not as other girls are."
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_13.png

name: Bell, Lexie
clubs: President of Y. W. C. A., '07-'08
Vice President of L. I. Alumni Association
description: Too august to be understood. Some of the underclassmen who have not learned better, venture the opinion that she has a history. One little slanderer even suggested that she had lost a lover in the Civil War. Such thoughts wound us.
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_13.png

name: Jernigen, William J.
motto: "Bill is tall, a brunette with eyebrows highly elevated and pursed lips. He wears a white hat in the summertime and dark trousers in the winter. He is the editor of the 'Weakly,' but can't help it. Bill has sleigh bells for eyes."
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_13.png

name: Spencer, Aileen
motto: "True, she is a fisher, but she angles for hearts."
clubs: Member of Y. W. C. A. Cabinet, '07-'08
description: How can a woman be so cruel as to break the heart of a poor, innocent man? Four, I have been told, lie prostrate at her feet. The mere glance of her eye is laden with Cupid's darts. Just the other day a down-town druggist was overheard to say that within the past week he had sold four bottles of deadly poison on trial, some of which were mailed to other points. 'Tis a secret, mind you, but I accidentally heard that Miss Spencer has received a letter bordered in black and containing the threatening words, "Every dog has his day."
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_13.png

name: Westbrook, Howell L.
motto: "A small, nice looking boy who lives on 'Lovers Lane.' 'Toady' doesn't believe in carrying too much work. 'Alexander the Great died at the age of 33 from overwork' he says. 'Toady' is one of the most popular young men in school."
image: 页面提取自－University of Arkansas_1909.pdf_页面_13.png
